ashtakoota.band_basis: Classical convention (panchanga practice): below 18 of 36 the match is traditionally not recommended; 18-24 average; 25-32 good; 33-36 excellent. A label, not a verdict.

bhakoot_dosha.basis: Aquarius and Sagittarius stand 3/11 from each other
bhakoot_dosha.name: bhakoot_dosha
bhakoot_dosha.source: Bhakoot (Rasi) kuta: mutual Moon-sign positions; the complementary pairs 2/12 (dwirdwadasha), 5/9 (nava-pancham) and 6/8 (shadashtaka) are dosha (0 points), all other placements earn the full 7. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom Aquarius (shudra) x bride Sagittarius (kshatriya)
kootas.source: Varna kuta: Moon-sign varna by element (water=Brahmin, fire=Kshatriya, earth=Vaishya, air=Shudra); 1 point when the groom's varna is not below the bride's. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

kootas.basis: groom Aquarius (manava) x bride Sagittarius (chatushpada)
kootas.source: Vashya kuta: five-fold sign classification (chatushpada/manava/jalachara/vanachara/keeta) with the first/second-half split of Sagittarius and Capricorn; scored groom-class x bride-class by the standard five-class points matrix (incl. the half-point cells). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: bride->groom Sadhaka, groom->bride Pratyari (malefic taras: Vipat/Pratyari/Vadha)
kootas.source: Tara (Dina) kuta: count nakshatras inclusively between the Moons, mod 9; remainders 3 (Vipat), 5 (Pratyari) and 7 (Vadha) are malefic. Each direction contributes 1.5 of the 3 points. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka scoring.

kootas.basis: groom Purva Bhadrapada (lion, male) x bride Purva Ashadha (monkey, male)
kootas.source: Yoni kuta: each nakshatra's animal (and sex) per the classical yoni table; scored by the standard 14x14 points matrix (same animal 4, sworn-enemy pair 0). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom lord saturn sees bride lord jupiter as neutral; bride lord sees groom lord as neutral
kootas.source: Graha Maitri kuta: natural (naisargika) relationship between the two Moon-sign lords per the BPHS maitri table (reused from almamesh.strength.friendship); scored friend/friend 5, friend/neutral 4, neutral/neutral 3, friend/enemy 1, neutral/enemy 0.5, enemy/enemy 0 (same lord counts as friend/friend). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka scoring.

kootas.basis: groom Purva Bhadrapada (manushya) x bride Purva Ashadha (manushya)
kootas.source: Gana kuta: nakshatra gana classes (deva/manushya/rakshasa) scored by the standard groom-x-bride matrix (same gana 6; deva groom + manushya bride 6; manushya groom + deva bride 5; rakshasa groom + deva bride 1; deva/manushya with rakshasa otherwise 0). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: Aquarius and Sagittarius stand 3/11 from each other
kootas.source: Bhakoot (Rasi) kuta: mutual Moon-sign positions; the complementary pairs 2/12 (dwirdwadasha), 5/9 (nava-pancham) and 6/8 (shadashtaka) are dosha (0 points), all other placements earn the full 7. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom Purva Bhadrapada (adi nadi) x bride Purva Ashadha (madhya nadi)
kootas.source: Nadi kuta: nakshatra nadi classes (adi/madhya/antya); different nadis earn the full 8 points, the same nadi is Nadi dosha (0 points) — the gravest Melapaka dosha. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

nadi_dosha.basis: groom Purva Bhadrapada (adi nadi) x bride Purva Ashadha (madhya nadi)
nadi_dosha.name: nadi_dosha
nadi_dosha.source: Nadi kuta: nakshatra nadi classes (adi/madhya/antya); different nadis earn the full 8 points, the same nadi is Nadi dosha (0 points) — the gravest Melapaka dosha. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

ashtakoota.source: Ashtakoota Guna Milan (36 gunas) per the standard Melapaka tradition — B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha (Electional Astrology)', Kuta agreement; the table behind every koota is cited on its own row.

1972-03-10T08:15:00+00:00|2000-12-31T23:.integrity_note: Relations are computed FROM two finished natal charts (read-only inputs); neither chart is recomputed, reweighted or mutated by the other.

a.convention: Mars in whole-sign houses 1/2/4/7/8/12 from each reference; the 2nd house is the South-Indian inclusion (labeled). has_dosha applies the strictest screening convention: a net dosha under ANY of Lagna/Moon/Venus.

cancellations.description: Mars stands in its own sign (Aries)
cancellations.source: Classical chart-computable exceptions (standard Muhurta compilation; cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha'): Mars in its own sign (Aries/Scorpio) or exalted (Capricorn) causes no dosha; the five-fold sign-house exception — Aries in the 1st, Scorpio in the 4th, Capricorn in the 7th, Cancer in the 8th, Sagittarius in the 12th (stated from the Lagna).

cancellations.description: Mars in Aries in the 1th — classical five-fold exception
cancellations.source: Classical chart-computable exceptions (standard Muhurta compilation; cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha'): Mars in its own sign (Aries/Scorpio) or exalted (Capricorn) causes no dosha; the five-fold sign-house exception — Aries in the 1st, Scorpio in the 4th, Capricorn in the 7th, Cancer in the 8th, Sagittarius in the 12th (stated from the Lagna).

references.school: from the Lagna — the classical reference (all schools)
references.source: Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

references.school: from the Lagna — the classical reference (all schools)

Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

mangal_match.basis: chart a clear, chart b afflicted
mangal_match.source: Mutual Kuja-dosha cancellation: when BOTH charts carry the dosha it stands neutralized between them — standard Muhurta practice; cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya.

contacts.source: Graha drishti per the Parashari whole-sign aspects (BPHS drishti adhyaya; offsets reused from almamesh.transits.aspects). Same-sign yuti is the classical conjunction; the 3.0 deg close-conjunction orb is a modern convention (heuristic).

significators_a.house_basis: 7th — vyapara, trade dealings and partnership (BPHS/Phaladeepika 7th significations; standard convention for business relations)

significators_b.house_basis: 7th — vyapara, trade dealings and partnership (BPHS/Phaladeepika 7th significations; standard convention for business relations)

synchrony.basis: Pure interval intersection over the engine-emitted maha+antar dates of both charts; segments exist only where both dated trees cover the window.

ashtakoota.band_basis: Classical convention (panchanga practice): below 18 of 36 the match is traditionally not recommended; 18-24 average; 25-32 good; 33-36 excellent. A label, not a verdict.

bhakoot_dosha.basis: Virgo and Leo stand 2/12 from each other

kootas.basis: bride->groom Vipat, groom->bride Maitra (malefic taras: Vipat/Pratyari/Vadha)

kootas.basis: groom Hasta (buffalo, female) x bride Purva Phalguni (rat, female)
kootas.source: Yoni kuta: each nakshatra's animal (and sex) per the classical yoni table; scored by the standard 14x14 points matrix (same animal 4, sworn-enemy pair 0). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

a_in_b.convention: Placements and drishti are whole-sign (classical); close_conjunction = same sign AND angular separation <= 3.0 deg — a modern orb convention, flagged heuristic on the contact.

significators_a.house_basis: 7th — kalatra bhava, marriage and union (BPHS house significations)

karakas.source: Jupiter — karaka of the husband in a woman's chart (stri-jataka tradition); emitted for both roles, the caller interprets by role

significators_b.house_basis: 7th — kalatra bhava, marriage and union (BPHS house significations)

karakas.source: Jupiter — karaka of the husband in a woman's chart (stri-jataka tradition); emitted for both roles, the caller interprets by role

synchrony.basis: Pure interval intersection over the engine-emitted maha+antar dates of both charts; segments exist only where both dated trees cover the window.

ashtakoota.band_basis: Classical convention (panchanga practice): below 18 of 36 the match is traditionally not recommended; 18-24 average; 25-32 good; 33-36 excellent. A label, not a verdict.

bhakoot_dosha.basis: Pisces and Libra stand 6/8 from each other
bhakoot_dosha.name: bhakoot_dosha
bhakoot_dosha.source: Bhakoot (Rasi) kuta: mutual Moon-sign positions; the complementary pairs 2/12 (dwirdwadasha), 5/9 (nava-pancham) and 6/8 (shadashtaka) are dosha (0 points), all other placements earn the full 7. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom Pisces (brahmin) x bride Libra (shudra)
kootas.source: Varna kuta: Moon-sign varna by element (water=Brahmin, fire=Kshatriya, earth=Vaishya, air=Shudra); 1 point when the groom's varna is not below the bride's. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

kootas.basis: groom Pisces (jalachara) x bride Libra (manava)
kootas.source: Vashya kuta: five-fold sign classification (chatushpada/manava/jalachara/vanachara/keeta) with the first/second-half split of Sagittarius and Capricorn; scored groom-class x bride-class by the standard five-class points matrix (incl. the half-point cells). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: bride->groom Kshema, groom->bride Vadha (malefic taras: Vipat/Pratyari/Vadha)
kootas.source: Tara (Dina) kuta: count nakshatras inclusively between the Moons, mod 9; remainders 3 (Vipat), 5 (Pratyari) and 7 (Vadha) are malefic. Each direction contributes 1.5 of the 3 points. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka scoring.

kootas.basis: groom Revati (elephant, female) x bride Swati (buffalo, male)
kootas.source: Yoni kuta: each nakshatra's animal (and sex) per the classical yoni table; scored by the standard 14x14 points matrix (same animal 4, sworn-enemy pair 0). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom lord jupiter sees bride lord venus as enemy; bride lord sees groom lord as neutral
kootas.source: Graha Maitri kuta: natural (naisargika) relationship between the two Moon-sign lords per the BPHS maitri table (reused from almamesh.strength.friendship); scored friend/friend 5, friend/neutral 4, neutral/neutral 3, friend/enemy 1, neutral/enemy 0.5, enemy/enemy 0 (same lord counts as friend/friend). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka scoring.

kootas.basis: groom Revati (deva) x bride Swati (deva)
kootas.source: Gana kuta: nakshatra gana classes (deva/manushya/rakshasa) scored by the standard groom-x-bride matrix (same gana 6; deva groom + manushya bride 6; manushya groom + deva bride 5; rakshasa groom + deva bride 1; deva/manushya with rakshasa otherwise 0). B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; matrix as tabulated in standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: Pisces and Libra stand 6/8 from each other
kootas.source: Bhakoot (Rasi) kuta: mutual Moon-sign positions; the complementary pairs 2/12 (dwirdwadasha), 5/9 (nava-pancham) and 6/8 (shadashtaka) are dosha (0 points), all other placements earn the full 7. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka practice.

kootas.basis: groom Revati (antya nadi) x bride Swati (antya nadi)
kootas.source: Nadi kuta: nakshatra nadi classes (adi/madhya/antya); different nadis earn the full 8 points, the same nadi is Nadi dosha (0 points) — the gravest Melapaka dosha. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

nadi_dosha.basis: groom Revati (antya nadi) x bride Swati (antya nadi)
nadi_dosha.name: nadi_dosha
nadi_dosha.source: Nadi kuta: nakshatra nadi classes (adi/madhya/antya); different nadis earn the full 8 points, the same nadi is Nadi dosha (0 points) — the gravest Melapaka dosha. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', Kuta agreement; standard panchanga Melapaka table.

ashtakoota.source: Ashtakoota Guna Milan (36 gunas) per the standard Melapaka tradition — B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha (Electional Astrology)', Kuta agreement; the table behind every koota is cited on its own row.

2010-06-21T18:00:00+00:00|2019-11-09T17:.integrity_note: Relations are computed FROM two finished natal charts (read-only inputs); neither chart is recomputed, reweighted or mutated by the other.

a.convention: Mars in whole-sign houses 1/2/4/7/8/12 from each reference; the 2nd house is the South-Indian inclusion (labeled). has_dosha applies the strictest screening convention: a net dosha under ANY of Lagna/Moon/Venus.

references.school: from the Lagna — the classical reference (all schools)
references.source: Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

b.convention: Mars in whole-sign houses 1/2/4/7/8/12 from each reference; the 2nd house is the South-Indian inclusion (labeled). has_dosha applies the strictest screening convention: a net dosha under ANY of Lagna/Moon/Venus.

references.school: from the Lagna — the classical reference (all schools)

Kuja (Mangal) dosha per the classical Muhurta tradition — Mars in houses 1/4/7/8/12 from the reference (cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya); the 2nd house and the Moon/Venus references per standard South-Indian practice.

mangal_match.basis: chart a afflicted, chart b afflicted
mangal_match.source: Mutual Kuja-dosha cancellation: when BOTH charts carry the dosha it stands neutralized between them — standard Muhurta practice; cf. B.V. Raman, 'Muhurtha', marriage adyaya.

b_in_a.convention: Placements and drishti are whole-sign (classical); close_conjunction = same sign AND angular separation <= 3.0 deg — a modern orb convention, flagged heuristic on the contact.

significators_a.house_basis: 11th — labha bhava, gains and the circle of friends (BPHS house significations)

karakas.source: Jupiter — karaka of the 11th house (labha/friends) per BPHS karakatva

significators_b.house_basis: 11th — labha bhava, gains and the circle of friends (BPHS house significations)

karakas.source: Jupiter — karaka of the 11th house (labha/friends) per BPHS karakatva

synchrony.basis: Pure interval intersection over the engine-emitted maha+antar dates of both charts; segments exist only where both dated trees cover the window.
